title: cracca constricta
intro: Cracca constricta, also known as the Narrow-leaved Bush-pea or Bush-pea, is a perennial legume native to Australia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as menstrual cramps, headaches, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and pods are used in herbal preparations, often in the form of teas, infusions, and poultices, to provide relief from pain and inflammation. Consuming the plant's leaves and pods may also offer health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress and promoting cardiovascular well-being.
